threshold_count: Restriction of energy intake + intense fear of weight gain + body image distortion

symptoms.description: The patient severely restricts food intake, leading to a markedly reduced body weight that is less than minimally expected for their age and height.
The patient exhibits an intense fear of gaining weight or becoming fat, or persistently behaves in ways that interfere with weight gain.
The patient has a distorted self-perception of being overweight despite actually being severely underweight.

threshold_count: Eating in binges followed by inappropriate compensatory behavior

symptoms.description: The patient recurrently consumes an objectively large amount of food in a discrete period of time, accompanied by a sense of lack of control over eating.
The patient experiences a sense of loss of control over eating during a binge episode.
The patient recurrently engages in inappropriate compensatory behaviors, such as self-induced vomiting, misuse of laxatives, or excessive exercise, to prevent weight gain.
The patient's self-evaluation is unduly influenced by their body shape and weight.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Binges plus 3 or more behavioral symptoms (e.g., eating fast, eating until uncomfortably full)

symptoms.description: The patient experiences recurrent episodes of binge eating involving large quantities of food.
The patient experiences a sense of loss of control over eating during a binge episode.
The patient eats much more rapidly than normal during a binge eating episode.
The patient eats until feeling uncomfortably full during a binge eating episode.
The patient eats large amounts of food when not feeling physically hungry.
The patient eats alone because of feeling embarrassed by how much they are eating during a binge episode.
The patient feels disgusted with themselves, depressed, or very guilty after an episode of binge eating.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Persists in eating material that is not food
duration_rule: 1 month or more; patient is at least 2 years old

symptoms.description: The patient persistently eats dirt, paper, soap, or other non-nutritional, non-food substances.

threshold_count: Regurgitating and re-chewing food already eaten

symptoms.description: The patient persistently regurgitates food, which may then be re-chewed, re-swallowed, or spit out.

threshold_count: Failure to meet nutrition/energy needs + at least 1 of 4 specified consequences

symptoms.description: The patient persistently avoids or restricts food intake based on a lack of interest, sensory characteristics of food, or concern about aversive consequences of eating.
The patient exhibits significant weight loss or a failure to achieve expected weight gain due to food avoidance.
The patient experiences a significant nutritional deficiency as a result of restrictive food intake.
The patient is dependent on enteral feeding or oral nutritional supplements to meet their nutritional needs.
The patient experiences marked interference with psychosocial functioning as a result of food avoidance.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Other Specified Feeding or Eating Disorder
threshold_count: Clinically important symptoms that fall below threshold for defined disorders
duration_rule: Variable (e.g., atypical anorexia, low frequency binges)

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits clinically important eating symptoms that fall below the diagnostic threshold for specific eating disorders.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Eating/feeding symptoms present but insufficient information for a specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits eating or feeding symptoms, but there is insufficient information to make a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.
